title: Caregiver – Manage & View PwD Profiles - Functional Test Plan
overview: Comprehensive functional testing of the Manage & View PwD Profiles feature on the AT/AD platform. Covers the Care Network Dashboard (PwD list, summary cards, limit/slots), PwD Summary Card interactions (View Profile, visual indicators for disability type/percentage/UDID, UI differentiation between PwD cards and Add PwD tile), Full PwD Profile view (read-only personal/contact/disability/education/Aadhaar/UDID details, auto-calculated age), Activity History view (device views, saved items, engagement timestamps, empty state), and edge cases (empty dashboard, partial data placeholders, network failure with retry).

inScope: Care Network Dashboard: PwD list, summary cards (Name, Disability type, Mobile, Email, Date added)
Dashboard: PwD limit display, used vs available slots, Add PwD button enablement
PwD Summary Card: View Profile CTA, visual indicators (disability type, disability percentage, UDID)
UI differentiation between PwD cards and Add PwD tile
Full PwD Profile: read-only details (Name, Age, Gender, Contact, Disability, Education, Aadhaar masked, UDID)
Age auto-calculated from Date of Birth
Activity History: device views, saved items, engagement timestamps
Activity History: empty-state message when no activity
Edge cases: empty dashboard, partial data, network failure
outOfScope: PwD limit enforcement details (covered in SCRUM-375)

testCases.title: Verify Care Network Dashboard displays PwD list with summary cards
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is logged in with at least 2 PwDs registered
steps: 1. Log in as caregiver
2. Navigate to My PwDs / Care Network Dashboard
3. Verify the page displays a list of all PwDs added by the caregiver
4. Verify each PwD is shown as a summary card
5. Verify each card contains: Name
6. Verify each card contains: Disability type
7. Verify each card contains: Mobile number
8. Verify each card contains: Email ID
9. Verify each card contains: Date added
10. Verify PwD limit is displayed (e.g., 5 max)
11. Verify used vs available slots are shown
12. Verify Add PwD button is enabled (since slots are available)
expectedResults: Dashboard loads with list of all caregiver's PwDs
Each PwD is displayed as a summary card
Cards contain Name, Disability type, Mobile, Email, Date added
PwD limit and used/available slots are displayed
Add PwD button is enabled when slots are available
testCases.postconditions: Dashboard is displayed with accurate PwD information

testCases.title: Verify PwD summary card visual indicators for disability type, percentage, and UDID
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is logged in; PwDs with disability percentage and UDID data exist
steps: 1. Navigate to Care Network Dashboard
2. Locate a PwD card with complete data
3. Verify visual indicator for Disability type is displayed (tag, icon, or badge)
4. Verify visual indicator for Disability percentage is displayed
5. Verify visual indicator for UDID is displayed (if PwD has UDID)
6. Verify indicators match the Figma design specification
7. Check a PwD card without UDID
8. Verify UDID indicator is absent or shows 'N/A' for PwD without UDID
expectedResults: Disability type visual indicator is clearly visible on card
Disability percentage indicator is displayed
UDID indicator is shown for PwDs with UDID
UDID indicator is absent or shows placeholder for PwDs without UDID
Indicators match Figma design
testCases.postconditions: Visual indicators on PwD cards are verified

testCases.title: Verify UI differentiates between PwD cards and Add PwD tile
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is logged in with PwDs below the limit
steps: 1. Navigate to Care Network Dashboard
2. Identify PwD summary cards
3. Identify the Add PwD tile/card
4. Verify PwD cards and Add PwD tile are visually distinct
5. Verify different styling (color, border, icon, layout) between the two
6. Verify Add PwD tile has a clear CTA (e.g., '+' icon, 'Add PwD' text)
7. Verify PwD cards show data while Add PwD tile shows action prompt
expectedResults: PwD cards and Add PwD tile are clearly visually distinct
Different styling differentiates the two types
Add PwD tile has clear action prompt
No confusion between data cards and action tile

testCases.title: Verify View Profile opens read-only PwD details with all fields
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is logged in; At least one PwD with complete data exists
steps: 1. Navigate to Care Network Dashboard
2. Click 'View Profile' on a PwD card
3. Verify PwD profile page loads
4. Verify Personal details are displayed: Name
5. Verify Age is displayed (auto-calculated from DOB)
6. Verify Gender is displayed
7. Verify Contact details are displayed (mobile, email)
8. Verify Disability details are displayed (type, percentage)
9. Verify Education details are displayed (qualification)
10. Verify Aadhaar is displayed in masked format (e.g., XXXX-XXXX-0123)
11. Verify UDID is displayed (if provided)
12. Verify all fields are read-only (non-editable)
13. Verify disability tags are clearly visible
expectedResults: Profile page loads with all PwD details
Name, Age, Gender are displayed correctly
Age is auto-calculated from DOB (e.g., 30 years)
Contact details (mobile, email) are shown
Disability and education details are shown
Aadhaar is masked for privacy
UDID is shown if available
All fields are read-only
Disability tags are clearly visible
testCases.postconditions: Full PwD profile view is verified

testCases.title: Verify Age is auto-calculated from Date of Birth
testCases.preconditions: PwD profile is open; PwD has DOB set to '1995-06-15'
steps: 1. Open PwD profile via View Profile
2. Locate the Age field
3. Verify Age is displayed as a calculated value (not a stored field)
4. Verify the age matches the expected calculation from DOB to current date
5. Check another PwD with a different DOB
6. Verify age is correctly calculated for that PwD as well
expectedResults: Age is auto-calculated from DOB, not manually entered
Age calculation is accurate based on current date
Different PwDs show correct individual ages

testCases.title: Verify Aadhaar is displayed masked in PwD profile
testCases.preconditions: PwD profile is open; PwD has Aadhaar number
steps: 1. Open PwD profile via View Profile
2. Locate the Aadhaar field
3. Verify Aadhaar is displayed in masked format (e.g., XXXX-XXXX-0123)
4. Verify the full 12-digit number is NOT visible
5. Verify the field is read-only
6. Attempt to select/copy the Aadhaar value
7. Verify privacy is maintained
expectedResults: Aadhaar is masked (only last 4 digits visible or similar)
Full number is not exposed
Field is read-only
Privacy is maintained
testCases.postconditions: Aadhaar masking in profile is verified

testCases.title: Verify Activity History view with device views, saved items, and timestamps
testCases.preconditions: PwD profile is open; PwD has activity history (device views, saved items)
steps: 1. Open PwD profile via View Profile
2. Verify 'Full Profile' tab/view is active by default
3. Locate the 'Activity History' tab/toggle
4. Click 'Activity History'
5. Verify the view switches to Activity History
6. Verify device views are listed with details
7. Verify saved items are listed
8. Verify engagement timestamps are displayed for each activity
9. Verify activities are in chronological order (most recent first)
10. Switch back to 'Full Profile'
11. Verify profile details are displayed again
expectedResults: Full Profile is the default view
Activity History tab/toggle is available
Switching shows activity history content
Device views are listed with details
Saved items are listed
Engagement timestamps are displayed
Switching back to Full Profile works correctly

testCases.title: Verify Activity History empty state when no activity exists
testCases.preconditions: PwD profile is open; PwD has NO activity history
steps: 1. Open profile of a PwD with no activity history
2. Switch to Activity History view
3. Verify an empty-state message is displayed
4. Verify the message is clear (e.g., 'No activity yet' or similar)
5. Verify the page layout is not broken by empty state
6. Verify the switch back to Full Profile works
expectedResults: Empty-state message is displayed when no activity exists
Message is clear and informative
Page layout remains intact
Tab switching still works correctly
testCases.postconditions: Activity History empty state is verified

testCases.title: Verify all profile fields are read-only (non-editable)
testCases.preconditions: PwD profile is open via View Profile
steps: 1. Open PwD profile
2. Attempt to click on Name field and type
3. Verify field does not accept input
4. Attempt to modify Age field
5. Verify field is non-editable
6. Attempt to modify Gender, Contact, Disability fields
7. Verify all fields are read-only
8. Verify no 'Edit' or 'Save' buttons are present on the profile view
expectedResults: All profile fields are read-only
No field accepts user input
No Edit/Save buttons are present
Fields are visually indicated as non-editable

testCases.title: Verify empty dashboard shows 'Add Your First PwD' state
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is logged in with zero PwDs
steps: 1. Log in as caregiver with no PwDs
2. Navigate to Care Network Dashboard
3. Verify empty state is displayed
4. Verify message: 'Add Your First PwD' or similar
5. Verify Add PwD button/tile is visible and enabled
6. Verify PwD limit and slots are still displayed (0/5)
expectedResults: Empty state with 'Add Your First PwD' message is displayed
Add PwD button is visible and enabled
Limit and slot information is shown (0/5)
No broken UI elements

testCases.title: Verify PwD card with partial data shows placeholders
testCases.preconditions: A PwD exists with missing email or phone number
steps: 1. Navigate to Care Network Dashboard
2. Locate the PwD card with partial data
3. Verify the card displays without errors
4. Verify missing fields show placeholders (e.g., 'N/A', '-')
5. Verify card layout is not broken
6. Click View Profile on the partial data card
7. Verify profile page handles missing data gracefully
expectedResults: Card displays without errors despite missing data
Placeholders shown for missing fields
Card layout remains intact
Profile page handles missing data gracefully

testCases.title: Verify network/API failure shows graceful error with retry option
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is logged in; Network is unstable or API is simulated to fail
steps: 1. Navigate to Care Network Dashboard
2. Simulate network failure or API error
3. Verify a graceful error message is displayed
4. Verify a retry option is provided
5. Restore network/API
6. Click retry
7. Verify dashboard loads correctly after retry
expectedResults: Graceful error message is displayed on failure
Retry option is provided
After retry with restored connection, dashboard loads correctly
No unhandled errors or blank screens

testCases.title: Verify PwD profile displays UDID when provided and hides when not
testCases.preconditions: Two PwDs exist: one with UDID, one without
steps: 1. Open profile of PwD with UDID
2. Verify UDID field is displayed with the value
3. Navigate back to dashboard
4. Open profile of PwD without UDID
5. Verify UDID field is hidden or shows 'Not provided'
6. Verify profile layout is not broken in either case
expectedResults: UDID is displayed when provided
UDID is hidden or shows placeholder when not provided
Profile layout is intact in both cases

AC1_care_network_dashboard.description: Dashboard displays PwD list, summary cards (Name, Disability, Mobile, Email, Date), limit, slots, Add button

AC2_summary_card_interactions.description: View Profile CTA, visual indicators (disability type/percentage/UDID), UI differentiation

AC3_view_full_profile.description: Read-only details (Name, Age, Gender, Contact, Disability, Education, Aadhaar masked, UDID), auto-calculated age, disability tags

AC4_activity_history.description: Switch between Full Profile and Activity History, device views, saved items, timestamps, empty state

EC_empty_dashboard.description: No PwDs added → Show empty state with 'Add Your First PwD'

EC_network_failure.description: Network/API failure → Graceful error with retry option

assumptions: Caregiver registration and sign-in are functional (SCRUM-357, SCRUM-360)
PwD registration is functional for creating test PwDs (SCRUM-372)
PwD limit is configured as 5 in QA
Activity history data exists for at least one PwD in QA
Figma design is available for visual indicator verification
PwDs with varying data completeness are available
dependencies: SCRUM-363: My PwDs dashboard must be functional
SCRUM-372: PwD registration for creating test data
SCRUM-375: PwD limit enforcement for slot display
SCRUM-381: PwD deletion for slot recovery testing

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: What specific visual indicators are used for disability type, percentage, and UDID on cards?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects TC_PROFILE_002 - need Figma reference for exact design

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: How is Age displayed - as years only, or years and months?
What activity types are tracked in Activity History beyond device views and saved items?
Is Activity History paginated or infinite scroll for PwDs with extensive activity?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: May need additional test case for pagination

priority_order: Highest tests (TC_PROFILE_001, 002, 004, 007) first - dashboard, card indicators, full profile, activity history
High priority tests (003, 005, 006, 008, 009, 010) - UI differentiation, age calc, Aadhaar masking, empty states, read-only
Medium priority tests (011, 012, 013) - partial data, network failure, UDID conditional display

estimated_effort.critical_path: 5-7 hours for critical path testing only

test_environment_requirements: QA environment with AT/AD platform accessible
Caregiver accounts with varying PwD counts and data completeness
PwDs with activity history data for Activity History testing
PwDs with and without UDID for conditional display testing
Figma design access for visual indicator verification

metadata.coverage: 100% of acceptance criteria (1-5) and all edge cases covered
